23.reason: [The recursive call returns a new node, but the returned pointer is not assigned to T->lchild, so the left subtree is never updated.]

reasons.buggy-qwen3-max-sc-1: 0.3333333333333333 , reason:  [The recursive call returns a new node, but the returned pointer is not assigned to T->lchild, so the left subtree is never updated.]
reasons.buggy-qwen3-max-sc-2: 0.3333333333333333 , reason:  [The recursive call returns a new node, but the result is not assigned to T->lchild, so the left subtree is never updated]
reasons.buggy-qwen3-max-sc-3: 0.3333333333333333 , reason:  [The recursive call does not update T->lchild, so the newly created node is lost. Should assign the result back to T->lchild.]
reasons.buggy-qwen3-max-sc-4: 0.3333333333333333 , reason:  [The recursive call does not update T->lchild, so the newly created node is lost. Should assign the result back: T->lchild = insert(T->lchild, data, dep + 1);]
reasons.buggy-qwen3-max-sc-5: 0.3333333333333333 , reason:  [The recursive call returns a new node, but the returned pointer is not assigned to T->lchild, so the left subtree is never updated.]

25.reason: [Same issue: the returned pointer from the recursive call is not assigned to T->rchild, so the right subtree is never updated.]

reasons.buggy-qwen3-max-sc-1: 0.3333333333333333 , reason:  [Same issue: the returned pointer from the recursive call is not assigned to T->rchild, so the right subtree is never updated.]
reasons.buggy-qwen3-max-sc-2: 0.3333333333333333 , reason:  [The recursive call returns a new node, but the result is not assigned to T->rchild, so the right subtree is never updated]
reasons.buggy-qwen3-max-sc-3: 0.3333333333333333 , reason:  [The recursive call does not update T->rchild, so the newly created node is lost. Should assign the result back to T->rchild.]
reasons.buggy-qwen3-max-sc-4: 0.3333333333333333 , reason:  [Same issue: missing assignment. Should be T->rchild = insert(T->rchild, data, dep + 1);]
reasons.buggy-qwen3-max-sc-5: 0.3333333333333333 , reason:  [Similarly, the returned pointer from the right recursive call is not assigned to T->rchild, so the right subtree is never updated.]
